metadata.title:
FOR JUDGEMENT of LABOR

Special Correspondence to The Press

pages.raw_text:
Special Correspondence Co The Press
WASHINGTON, D. c., July 81
Both parties have spoken
choose between them," is the cam
paign slogan sounded by Pres.
Gompers of the American Fedora
tion of Labor. He refers particu-
larly to the injunction planks of
the republican and democratic
platforms. There is a sharp pop.
ping of controversy over the mat.
ter among labor men in all parts
of the country. Appended are the
planks submitted by the two par.
ties for the judgment of labor:


DEMOCRATIC


The courts of justice are the
bulwark of our liberties, and we
yield to none in our purpose to
maintain their dignity. Our party
has given to the bench a long
list of distinguished judges, who
have added to the respect and
confidence in which this depart
ment must be jealousy main
tame. We resent the attempt of
the republican party to raise
false issues respecting the judi
ciary. 1t is an unjust reflection
upon a great body of our citizens
to assume that they lack respect
for the courts.


1t is the function of the courts
to interpret the laws which the
people create, and if the laws APP
pear to work economic, social or
political injustice, it is our duty
to change them. The only basis
upon which the integrity of our
courts can stand is that of answer
ing justice and protection of life.
If judicial processes may be abused
we should guard them against
abuse.


Experience has proved the ne
cessity of a modification of the
present law relating to injunctions,
and We reiterate the pledge of our
national platforms of 1896 and 1900
in favor Of a measure which pass
ed the United States senate in
1896, but which a republican con-
gress has ever since refused to en-
act. relating to contempts in fed.
eral courts and providing for trial
by jury in cases of indirect con.
tempt.


Questions of judicial practice
have arisen. especially in Con
necticut, with industrial disputes
We deem that the parties to all
Judicial proceedings should be
treated with rigid impartiality. and
that injunctions should not be is
sued in any cases in which injunc
tions would not issue if no indus
trial dispute were involved.


The expanding organization OIL
industry makes it essential that
there should be no abridgment OF
the right of wage earners and
producers to organize for the pro
lection of wages and the improve
ment of labor conditions to the enc
that such labor organizations and
their members should not be re
